LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED AS TO EACH CAUSE OF ACTION. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS NOT SHOWN, AS A MATTER OF LAW, THAT THE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE BARRED BY THE LITIGATION PRIVILEGE. FURTHER, THIS ISSUE WAS ADDRESSED IN THE COURT'S ORDER OF SEPTEMBER 27, 2013, WHICH DENIED DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHETHER PLAINTIFF IS AN INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY TO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT INVOLVES A QUESTION OF FACT WHICH IS NOT A PROPER SUBJECT ON A DEMURRER. DEFENDANT HAS CITED NO APPLICABLE AUTHORITY IN ITS MOVING OR REPLY PAPERS THAT WOULD BAR PLAINTIFF FROM BRINGING A BREACH OF CONTRACT AGAINST ANOTHER ALLEGED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY TO THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED AS PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL INTERFERENCE OF A CONTRACT AGAINST DEFENDANT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A POSSESSORY INTEREST IN THE SETTLEMENT FUNDS IS A QUESTION OF FACT WHICH IS NOT PROPER SUBJECT ON DEMURRER. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER ON THE GROUNDS OF UNCERTAINTY IS OVERRULED. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

LAW AND MOTION, 302, RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANTS CJH & ASSOCIATES AND CHIJEH HU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. ALL REQUESTS FOR JUDICIAL NOTICE ARE GRANTED. DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. ALTHOUGH PLAINTIFF HAS NOT PLED FACTS DEMONSTRATING THE EXISTENCE OF A VALID ATTORNEY'S LIEN IN ACCORDANCE WITH CALIFORNIA RULES OF PROFESSIONAL CONDUCT 3-300, PLAINTIFF HAS ALLEGED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ESTABLISH DEFENDANTS' BREACH OF THE SETTLEMENT AGREEMENT, TO WHICH PLAINTIFF IS AN INTENDED THIRD PARTY BENEFICIARY. (SEE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT 22-25, EXH. 4; SEE ALSO QUELIMANE CO., INC. V. STEWART TITLE GUAR. CO. (1998) 19 CAL.4TH 26, 38-39 (HOLDING A PROPERLY PLED CAUSE OF ACTION UNDER ANY THEORY IS GOOD AGAINST GENERAL DEMURRER.)) DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. WHETHER PLAINTIFF HAS A RIGHT TO POSSESS THE SETTLEMENT FUNDS IS FACTUAL QUESTION WHICH IS NOT A PROPER SUBJECT ON DEMURRER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR5976

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Adjudication
Off Calendar Jan-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. AGUSTIN R. PINA of The Schinner Law Group, Tel No. (415) 369-9050 appearing for Plaintiff Michael G. Schinner dba The Schinner Law Group;
Atty. LORI C. HERSHORN of Hershorn & Henry, LLP, Tel No. (949) 859-5600 appearing for Defendant Hershorn & Henry, LLP.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP'S Motion for Summary Judgment is denied. DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP'S Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted and denied in part. The litigation privilege does not bar this action because plaintiff is complaining about defendant's conduct, not its publications or communications. See Rusheen v. Cohen (2006) 37 Cal.4th 1048, 1057, 1065. The court grants summary adjudication on the breach of contract cause of action. Even if the court assumes that both plaintiff and defendant H&H were third party beneficiaries under the Chicago Title/Zhang settlement agreement, there is no authority holding that plaintiff can sue defendant H&H for breach of contract. The parties to the underlying contract (Chicago Title and the Zhangs) were the promisors and obliged to perform the contract. The court denies summary adjudication on the intentional interference with contract cause of action. There are triable issues of material fact regarding defendant H&H's knowledge of the agreement, its conduct to cause the breach, and causation. The court denies summary adjudication on the conversion cause of action. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding plaintiff's ownership interest in the money. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: A. James Robertson, II; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/AJR)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: AGUSTIN PINA, ESQ., 415.369.9050, FOR PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SCHINNER.
EDWARD SCHROEDER, ESQ., 415.979.0400, FOR DEFENDANTS CHIJEH HU AND CJH & ASSOCIATES.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS CJH & ASSOCIATES AND CHIJEH HU'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendants' demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled. Although Plaintiff has not pled facts demonstrating the existence of a valid attorney's lien in accordance with California Rules of Professional Conduct 3-300, Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to establish Defendants' breach of the Settlement Agreement, to which Plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary. (See First Amended Complaint 22-25, Exh. 4; see also Quelimane Co., Inc. v. Stewart Title Guar. Co. (1998) 19 Cal.4th 26, 38-39 (holding a properly pled cause of action under any theory is good against general demurrer.)) Defendants' demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff has a right to possess the settlement funds is factual question which is not a proper subject on demurrer. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-30-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: AGUSTIN PINA, ESQ., 415.369.9050, FOR PLAINTIFF MICHAEL SCHINNER.
LORI HERSHORIN, ESQ., 949.859.5600, FOR DEFENDANT HERSHORIN & HENRY, LLP.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANT HERSHORN & HENRY, LLP'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to each cause of action. All requests for judicial notice are granted. Defendant has not shown, as a matter of law, that the causes of action are barred by the litigation privilege. Further, this issue was addressed in the Court's order of September 27, 2013, which denied Defendant's special motion to strike. Defendant's demurrer to the second cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff is an intended third party beneficiary to the Settlement Agreement involves a question of fact which is not a proper subject on a demurrer. Defendant has cited no applicable authority in its moving or reply papers that would bar Plaintiff from bringing a breach of contract against another alleged third party beneficiary to the Settlement Agreement. Defendant's demurrer to the third cause of action is overruled as Plaintiff has alleged sufficient facts to state a cause of action for intentional interference of a contract against Defendant. Defendant's demurrer to the fourth cause of action is overruled. Whether Plaintiff has a possessory interest in the settlement funds is a question of fact which is not proper subject on demurrer. Defendant's demurrer on the grounds of uncertainty is overruled. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest Goldsmith, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR5976
